observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The GSO and the Disposal Committee to attend trainings and seminars in the conduct of disposal of government properties in line with the new issuance of the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties;
The Disposal Committee to follow the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of disposal of unserviceable property provided in the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties; and
The Office of the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entries in the derecognition of the demolished facilities and unserviceable properties after disposal in the books and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendations to Management:
The Municipal Accountant review the item with negative balance and makes appropriate adjustments as necessary; and,
The Municipal Accountant reconcile all items within the accounts Due from and Due to Other Funds, effect the transfer of funds and provide complete and accurate information readily available to the users of information.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendations to management:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records and effect the necessary adjustments to avoid accumulation thereof and come up with the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account in their respective records;
The LCE to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile regularly their respective cash records together with the records of the depository banks pertaining to the cash accounts of the agency to detect instantly errors either by the bank, Accounting unit or Cash unit; and,
Evaluate the necessity of maintaining those bank accounts with minimal balances, particularly those falling below its maintaining balance to avoid possible bank charges, and assess the possibility of closing said accounts if warranted.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The department in-charge to give utmost priority in the registration of the 19 parcels of land owned by the LGU under its name in the Torrens Title System;
The Office of the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GSO in the reconciliation of land properties listed in the schedule of Land account in the financial statements with the source documents maintained by the GSO; and
The Office of the Municipal Accountant to provide relevant details on the disclosure of the schedule of Land account in the financial statements that can aid the departments, especially during the conduct of inventory, in identifying the land with the corresponding titles.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management consider the preparation of a request for write-off of the Other Receivable account which were considered dormant following the procedures and qualifications provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to Management:
The Municipal Accountant submit justifications on the discrepancies noted between the SCBAA and SAAOB; and,
Based on the SAAOB obtained from the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Accountant prepare the SCBAA diligently to avoid such discrepancies.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to prepare and include the required reconciliation in the Notes to Financial Statements for any material differences of actual amounts between SCBAA and the Statement of Financial Performance based on the information obtained from the Municipal Budget Officer.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer to endorse the reactivation of dormant accounts to the LGU’s Sangguniang Bayan (SB) for the issuance of SB resolution;
The Municipal Treasurer revert back the balances to the Bureau of Treasury, the Source Agency or the general fund, as the case may be; and,
The Municipal Treasurer monitor the activity or inactivity of its depository accounts and exert all efforts to prevent accounts from becoming dormant.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to Management:
The Municipal Accountant obtain the Bank Statements to determine the increase/decrease of the bank accounts;
The Municipal Accountant adjust the CIB-LCCA and record the corresponding income/expense, if any; and,
Management enroll all LBP bank accounts in the weAccess.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The General Services Office conduct physical count to verify the existence of the recorded biological assets and furnish the audit team the results of the physical count;
The Office of the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to retrieve any supporting documents relative to the livestock (Memorandum of Agreement, Distribution list, etc.) and furnish the audit team of the same; and
The Office of the Municipal Accountant provide journal entries for the derecognition of the livestock in the Trust Fund books and furnish the audit team of the Journal Entry Voucher with its supporting documents, if warranted.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations and, Management agreed to:
Provide the Audit Team with an update on the aforementioned abandoned projects particularly on its percentage of completion, progress payments made and plan of action of the LGU on the said projects; and,
Send communication both to the source agency and the contractor to clarify matters pertaining to the said projects and to gather data and/or information on the possible plan of action to resolve the issue.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Municipal Accountant:
Verify the validity of all recorded payables and make confirmations to check whether said claims actually exist or not especially those which are long outstanding and consider the reversion of those payables with no actual claimant after exhausting all necessary remedies to validate the existence of such obligations;
Payments of all payables with valid claimants and complete supporting documents be immediately made or processed in accordance with the applicable rules and regulations and upon the presumption that budgets for these obligations were already earmarked through the Obligation Request duly signed by the budget officer to avoid accumulation of unpaid obligations; and
Effect the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify other accounts erroneously booked in the Accounts Payable account to ensure that accounts are accurately presented in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Municipal Accountant:
Present the current and non-current portion of the Other Payables account in the Statement of Financial Position and the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements;
Reconcile the negative balance due to erroneous recording of remittances of Salary Loans; and,
Reclassify the Calamity Fund Reserve of Other Payables to Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:
The Municipal Accountant justify the deficiency noted in the BRS of the depository account; and,
The Municipal Accountant adjust the Cash in Bank-LCCA and record the Due to NGAs, accordingly.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management through the LDRRM Council ensure that only disaster risk reduction and management activities are included in its LDRRMF Investment Plan and the annual budget for the 5 per cent LDRRM Fund. In relation thereto, funding for administrative expenses, MOOE and Capital Outlay (CO) requirements of the LDRRM Office be included in the appropriations of said office under the Office of the Municipal Mayor.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management review attached supporting documents such as DTRs and Accomplishment Reports and strictly grant Hazard Pay to qualified employees only during periods of actual exposure to hazards and hardships in accordance to the provisions of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No.1, series of 2016.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Treasurer, ensure strict compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 92-239 in order to maintain a sound system of internal control in the disbursement of public funds. The perforation or stamping of the word “PAID” be made in the disbursement vouchers and all its supporting documents. It will likewise serve as a control measure in the prevention of the use of the same documents for claims of the same nature.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the LSB submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017) on SEF utilization, as prepared by the Local Accountant, to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG, within the timelines.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the LSB ensure the posting of the SEF Utilization Reports at the website of the LGU and/or in at least three conspicuous public places and that the same be made not later than the 20th day after the end of each quarter as required under DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2024-021.

Recommendation:
We recommended Management to submit the necessary reports as mentioned above and its corresponding supporting documents and/or compliance of requirements as indicated to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and that vouchers onwards be duly processed and approved before they will be paid to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts.
Also, institute as a matter of policy, that the quantity of fuel issued to every vehicle correspond only to the vehicle’s average consumption per distance of travel plus 10 per cent allowance.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records and effect the necessary adjustments to avoid accumulation thereof and come up with the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account in their respective records;
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile regularly their respective cash records together with the records of the depository banks pertaining to the cash accounts of the agency to detect instantly errors either by the bank, Accounting unit or Cash unit; and
Evaluate the necessity of maintaining those bank accounts with minimal balances, particularly those falling below its maintaining balance to avoid possible bank charges, and assess the possibility of closing said accounts if warranted.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:
The Municipal Accountant make immediate and appropriate action on the transfer of funds for these reciprocal accounts to its appropriate account in order to present reliable information particularly on the fund balances maintained by the LGU;
The Municipal Accountant review all items with negative balances and make appropriate adjustments as necessary; and
The Municipal Accountant reconcile all items within the accounts Due from and Due to Other Funds and provide complete and accurate information readily available to the users of information.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendations to management to:
Coordinate with the project coordinator/head to identify the beneficiaries;
Send demand letters to the beneficiaries of the loan granted; and
Determine the status of collectability of these accounts and consider the submission of request for write-off, if warranted, following the procedures and qualifications provided under COA Circular No. 2023-008 with consideration of its effectivity, that is, within one (1) year, the procedures for the one-time cleansing be undertaken.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:
Provide the Audit Team with an update on the aforementioned abandoned projects particularly on its percentage of completion, progress payments made and plan of action of the LGU on the said projects; and
Send communication both to the source agency and the contractor to clarify matters pertaining to the said projects and to gather data and/or information on the possible plan of action to resolve the issue.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:
That the Municipal Accountant properly present prior period errors and adjustments by restating the prior year balances of the affected accounts shown as corresponding figures for the current year FS in compliance with the requirements under Paragraphs 46 to 53 of IPSAS 3.
That the Municipal Accountant prepare appropriate disclosures in the Notes to FS pertaining to prior period errors and adjustments pursuant to Paragraph 54 of IPSAS 3.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:
The Municipal Accountant submit justifications on the discrepancies noted between the SCBAA and SAAOB; and
Based on the SAAOB obtained from the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Accountant prepare the SCBAA diligently to avoid such discrepancies.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to prepare and include the required reconciliation on the Notes to Financial Statements for any material differences between the actual and budgeted amounts based on the information obtained from the Municipal Budget Officer.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to timely submit the LGU’s year-end financial statements which is on or before the 14th day of February, and to discuss with the Municipal Accounting Office the causes of delay in the submission of year-end financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:
The GSO submit a written explanation on its failure to timely inform the Audit Team in cases where there are deliveries of procured items; and
The GSO furnish the COA Auditor the copies of delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance of goods and services so that timely inspection thereof can be conducted by the Auditors.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:
Refrain from procuring goods and services through Contract of Service/Job Order without undergoing the standard procurement procedures as required under RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR; and
Submit a written explanation to the Audit Team justifying the manner used by management in procuring the aforementioned goods and services through Contract of Service/Job Order.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the LDRRMO to submit immediately the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund for the CY 2023 and henceforth to submit the same on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor of the LGU. Furthermore, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRMO to make regular reconciliation of their records to ensure correctness of data and to avoid inconsistent information which may have a negative impact in the decision-making process of the LGU in the planning and utilization of its LDRRMF.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:
The Municipal Accountant, LDRRMO and Budget Officer reconcile their records to determine the correct balance of the CY 2023 unexpended LDRRMF QRF and MOOE that should be transferred to the Special rust Fund;
The Municipal Accountant transfer only the unexpended/unobligated LDRRMF QRF/MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under Trust Liability-DRRM account excluding the unexpended LDRRMF-CO which must remain in the general fund as a continuing appropriation; and
The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the LDRRM Officer and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the immediate transfer of any unexpended balance of LDRRMF-QRF/MOOE to the Special Trust Fund books and bank account.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and LDRRMO, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer, validate the balances of the Cash in Bank - LCCA for the LDRRM and the Trust Liability – DRRM account in the Trust Fund Books, and determine the correct amount of LDRRM funds in its depository bank.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management requires all accountable officers to ensure that collections be remitted or deposited/liquidated intact and daily or where collections are minimal and daily deposit thereof becomes costly and impractical, the collecting officer shall deposit her collections at least once a week, or as soon as the collections reach ₱5,000.00

Recommendation:
We recommended that the accountable officers regularly render their report of accountability for accountable forms at the end of each month for consolidation by the Municipal Treasurer.

Recommendation:
We recommended to Management that the Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Point System endorse to the Committee on Family, Women, Children and Elderly of the SB, the passing of an ordinance localizing RA No. 11313. Further, include in the ordinance the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions, b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof, and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the discrepancies noted and make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due To Other Funds accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

a)	The Local Chief Executive be guided by the guidelines and procedures of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts of the municipality;

b)	The inventory committee conduct the physical inventory count in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and prepare the Report of Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) duly approved by the Head of the Agency and distribute the same as follows:
	
Original	–	COA Auditor, through the Accounting Division/Unit
Copy 2	–	Accounting Division/Unit
Copy 3	–	Supply and/or Property Division/Unit
Copy 4	–	Inventory Officer/Committee

c)	The GSO and Accounting Units undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records;

d)	The GSO prepares the List of PPE found at station and Non-existing/Missing PPE using the format provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 based on the RCPPE and follow the procedures for the disposition of non-existing/ missing PPEs provided under Paragraph 7 of the Circular;

e)	The Accounting Unit take up the necessary accounting entries to recognize PPE found at station and to recognize loss of PPE and to set-up the corresponding receivables from the concerned accountable officer/personnel; and

f)	Ensure that items of PPE reflected in the agency’s FS are actually existing and if no complete information is available for the dropping of non-existent/missing properties, full disclosure must be made in the Notes to FS.

We recommended the following to management:

a)	The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the General Services Officer or concerned LGU personnel with information as to the status of LGU properties pertaining to its current purpose and use. Verify the actual status of the affected properties and make the adjusting entry to record the said properties to its appropriate accounts.

b)	The GSO or concerned personnel in-charged with the management of LGU properties immediately inform the Accounting Unit of any changes in its use in order for the latter to timely effect any adjustments in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the details of the LDRRM Fund using the prescribed presentation and format provided under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended the following to management:

a)	Prepare and submit a copy of the Report on Utilization of the BGCM to the Audit Team using the prescribed format (Annex C) of LBC No. 125 to monitor and track the remaining PPAs that are still on-going, if any; and

b)	Post the same in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the municipality.
observations.reason: This audit recommendation has been reiterated and included in Part II of this report under observation No. 15.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:

a)	Require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the reports and documents relative to the collections of and payments from government funds to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the Municipality otherwise the sanctions provided in Section 122 of P.D. No.1445 shall be imposed; and

b)	Submit justification for failure to submit the required reports and documents despite repeated demands.
observations.reason: This audit recommendation has been reiterated and included in Part II of this report under observation No. 7.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:

a)	Direct all concerned personnel to submit copies of POs to the GSO immediately upon perfection for monitoring purposes. It would also be appropriate if management would include in its procedures that all POs should be coursed through the GSO for control purposes; 

b)	The GSO retain a copy of the signed POs and submit to the COA Auditor within 5 working days, irrespective of amount, together with the related supporting documents; and

c)	The GSO furnish the COA Auditor with copies of delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance of goods and services.
observations.reason: This audit recommendation has been reiterated and included in Part II of this report under observation No. 8.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:

a)	Submit to the Auditor’s Office the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption so that the use of the LGU’s motor vehicle can be properly monitored/controlled and regulated as required in the regulations and the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period determined; and

b)	Explain in writing the possibility of the one-time acceptance, inspection, issuance and receipt of the large quantities of fuel despite the lack of Fuel Depot or storage facility to justify the occurrence of the transaction.

We recommended the following to management:

a)	The Local Chief Executive to

i.	submit documents pertaining to the unfinished projects to support the delays incurred in its completion, if any;

ii.	issue termination of contract to erring contractors for projects wherein they have incurred a negative slippage of ten percent (10%) or more in the completion of the work pursuant to Section III-A (2) of the Guidelines on Termination of Contracts; 

iii.	cause the forfeiture of the performance securities of contractors pursuant to Section 39.3 of the IRR of RA 9184; and

iv.	 issue a Blacklisting Order upon termination of contracts due to default of the contractors pursuant to Section 6 of the Uniform Guidelines for Blacklisting Manufacturers, Suppliers, Distributors, Contractors and Consultants.

b)	Municipal Engineer

i.	initiate action on the possible imposition of liquidated damages against delaying contractors pursuant to Section 8.5 of the Contract Implementation Guidelines for the Procurement of Infrastructure Projects; 

ii.	provide computation on the possible liquidated damages which the LGU may claim from the contractors; and

iii.	make sure that all infrastructure projects are strictly monitored in accordance with plans and schedules of implementation so that available actions or remedies can be imposed against erring contractors.

c)	Institute legal action against the contractor, if warranted.

We recommended the following to management:

a)	Prepare and submit a copy of the LDRRMFIP to the Audit Team using the prescribed format pursuant to paragraph 5.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-002;

b)	Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the duly certified Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and forward the same to the MDRRMO; and

c)	Require the MDRRMO to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council within the prescribed period, which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.reason: This audit recommendation has been reiterated and included in Part II of this report under observation No. 16.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:

a)	Require the LGU GFPS to conduct HGDG test in evaluating the agency’s PAPs gender sensitivity and responsiveness for client-focused activities and GMEF for organization-focused activities;

b)	Based on the HGDG score, provide a percentage of the annual budget of the regular program or project that may be attributed to the GAD budget and reflect it on the Annual GPB (Annex D), columns 8 to 12; and

c)	Determine the extent that the targeted HGDG score is attained in order to determine the actual expenditure attributable to the GAD budget and reflect it on the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report (Annex E), columns 10 to 14.

We recommended that management observe proper disclosures in the Notes to FS, particularly on reconciliation of cash and cash equivalents figure per SCF with that in the SFPos, reconciliation of net cash flows from operating activities to surplus (deficit), and reconciliation of budget and actual amounts with that in the SFP.
We recommended that management observe proper disclosure and exercise utmost diligence in the preparation and reporting of financial information.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the collections regularly and intact to the authorized depository bank so as to avoid misuse of government funds, pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on the NGAS-LGU. Further, for the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately look into these negative balances and make the necessary adjustments to correct the said deficiency.

Furthermore, all stages in the procurement process be faithfully adhered to in line with the commitment of the Government to promote good governance and its effort to adhere to the principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency, and economy in procurement.

We recommended that management:

b)	Submit the complete set of documents pertaining to the above-cited projects and of all other projects implemented in CY 2019 according to the sequence cited in the schedule above with proper tabbing for easy referencing; and

c)	Strictly observed the submission of required documents within the prescribed reglementary period for all future procurements for timely, systematic, and effective review and evaluation of the project.

We recommended the following:

b)	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary equipment ledger cards and subsidiary ledgers in support to the Subsidiary Ledger account classification and Trust Liability-DRRM account, respectively; and 

c)	Necessitate the Municipal Accountant to use the prescribed presentation of discussion of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that management:

a)	Strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely submission of the GPB and AR to the COA Audit Team;

b)	Require the GAD Focal Point System to: (i) institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; (ii) undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and (iii) revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues; and

c)	Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Management initiated actions in order to comply with the audit recommendations but failed to fully implement all recommendations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the said lacking documents in order to determine the propriety and veracity of the transaction. In addition, we further recommend that management observed proper internal control and stop allowing disbursements with lacking supporting documents.

We recommended the submission of the list of the newly created Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and a copy of the local government solid waste management plans to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation and verification of the systematic, comprehensive and ecological waste management program of the municipal government.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to record the liquidations made in relation to the various funds received from the National Governments Agencies. Deduct from the Due to NGA account the corresponding amounts already liquidated. We recommend further that management stop the practice of maintaining these funds as liabilities for a long period of time and observe utmost diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities concerning the preparation and reporting of financial information. Moreover, direct the accounting department to return to the source agency any unused balance of the funds upon completion as prescribed by the above cited provision.

We recommended that the accountable officers be directed to comply and render the above-said report to the COA unit auditor as prescribed in Sec. 98, of the GAAM Vol. I and prescribed forms provided under Section 50 and 51 of NGAS for LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended faithful adherence to the provisions of R.A. 9184 and its IRR in all procurement activities of the LGU. The persons responsible in the procurement are to ensure that the applicable provisions of RA 9184 for every procurement process are adhered to.

We recommended to require the Accountable Officers/Collecting Officers to post sufficient fidelity bond in accordance to the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 2-2009 dated August 6, 2009 commensurate to their cash accountability to provide sufficient protection and indemnification in cases of losses.

observations.recommendation: The BAC should prepare a procurement monitoring report to be submitted to the Municipal Mayor for approval and for submission to the GPPB within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester to monitor the proper implementation by procuring entities.

Recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant record the adjustments for the unrecorded reconciling items and inform the audit team of compliance thereof through submission of related documents such as Journal Entry Vouchers and its supporting documents; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile regularly their respective cash records together with the records of the depository banks pertaining to the cash accounts of the agency to detect instantly errors either by the bank, Accounting unit or Cash unit.
Management’s Comment:
Management has not yet submitted its written comment on this observation and recommendation. However, management responded during the exit conference that they will abide with the auditor’s recommendation.

Recommendation:

Send demand letters to the beneficiaries of the loan granted; and
Determine the status of collectability of these accounts and consider the submission of request for write-off, if warranted, following the procedures and qualifications provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
Management’s Comment:
Management has not yet submitted its written comment on this observation and recommendation. However, they responded during our exit conference that they will do their best to gather any document or information to determine the status of its collectability.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and all concerned personnel undertake immediate appropriate action to validate the status and existence of all PPEs and be guided by the guidelines and procedures of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts of the municipality to ensure that items of PPE reflected in the agency’s FS are actually existing, and if no complete information is available for the dropping of non-existent/missing properties.  In all these instances, full disclosures are to be made in the Notes to FS.
Management’s Comment:
Management has not yet submitted its written comment on this observation and recommendation. However, they acknowledged the noted deficiency during the exit conference and responded that they will follow the auditor’s recommendation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reclassify the amounts granted to the barangays from the account Subsidy to Other LGUs to Due from LGUs and require the recipient barangays to submit liquidation documents to reflect the correct balance of the aforementioned accounts in the books of the LGU;
Upon liquidation and when liquidation documents are complete and proper, record the same to its appropriate account depending on the agreement entered into by the parties as to whether who will own the completed project; and
Moving forward, record all succeeding fund transfers to other LGUs with specific purpose(s) to Due from LGUs and ensure that specific instructions are conveyed to recipient barangays to liquidate the amount received.
Management Comment:
Management has not yet submitted its written comment on this observation and recommendation. However, they acknowledged the noted deficiency during the exit conference and responded that they will follow the auditor’s recommendation.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:
Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the itemized details of the computation of investment property, prior period errors and adjustment of net revenue recognized directly in net assets/equity including its descriptive and explanatory information;
Reconcile the misbalance and inconsistent amounts; and
Conduct a comprehensive review of the FS before making it available to the users of the Financial Statements.
Management’s Comment:
Management has not yet submitted its written comment on this observation and recommendation. However, they acknowledged the noted deficiency during the exit conference and responded that they will follow the auditor’s recommendation. They were already in the process of making the necessary corrections and adjustments.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
We would like to request management that all necessary corrections and adjustment to the submitted FS be properly communicated to the audit team in writing.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following to management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the required reports and transaction documents within the reglementary period to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the Municipality otherwise the sanctions as provided in Section 122 of P.D. No.1445 shall be imposed;
Maintain a monitoring tool for DVs forwarded to the Treasurer’s office or Mayor’s office, as the case may be, for approval and check preparation, to easily monitor the movement and status of the DVs and have a ready record as basis for follow-ups; and
Submit justification for failure to submit the required reports and documents within the reglementary period.
Management’s Comment:
Management has not yet submitted its written comment on this observation and recommendation. However, they acknowledged the noted deficiency during the exit conference and responded that they will follow the auditor’s recommendation. They will initiate necessary adjustment on their current practices and aims to minimize, if not eliminate, the delays on their submissions.

The GSO retain a copy of the signed POs and submit to the COA Auditor within 5 working days, irrespective of amount, together with the related supporting documents; and
The GSO furnish the COA Auditor with copies of delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance of goods and services.
Management’s Comment:
Management has not yet submitted its written comment on this observation and recommendation. However, they acknowledged the noted deficiency during the exit conference and responded that they will follow the auditor’s recommendation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer:

Recommendation:

Management commented that the delay in the implementation of the development projects under the 20% LDF were due to some revisions of PPA’s in the prior year and the presence of health problems in the community. But rest assured that they will abide with all the recommendations and do their best to cope up with the implementation of the projects. Additionally, they will discuss the deficiencies with the Municipal Development Council to make some improvement especially in the preparation and formulation of the plan during the planning stage.

Submit a copy of the Report on Utilization of the BGCM to the Audit Team for purposes of validation; and
Post the same in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the municipality.
Management’s Comment:
Management has not yet submitted its written comment on this observation and recommendation. However, they acknowledged the noted deficiency during the exit conference and responded that they will follow the auditor’s recommendation.

With the recommendations being reiterated, the Planning Office thereby commit to abide and comply with the recommendation. In the same manner, other concerned offices acknowledged the audit observation and commit to comply with the auditor’s recommendation.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management require the BAC to submit a written explanation narrating the actual situation at that time in order to establish the grounds that will warrant the use of negotiated procurement under emergency cases as mentioned in Section 53.2 of revised IRR of RA 9184.
Management’s Comment:
Management acknowledged the noted deficiency and commented that the recommendation to use negotiated procurement-emergency cases was made as well as its justification, however, this was not properly documented as this was done verbally. The next time around, they will surely make the recommendation and justification in writing.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
We maintained our audit recommendation on the submission of a written explanation narrating the actual situation on the above-mentioned procurements. In resorting to Negotiated Procurement – Emergency Cases as the mode of procurement, it is emphasized that the request should always be supported with any document or explanation narrating the actual situation in order to establish the grounds that will warrant the use of negotiated procurement under emergency cases.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant prepare and submit to COA Office the monthly BRS within the reglementary period as required under COA Circular No. 96-011 and record the adjustments for any reconciling item;
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile regularly their respective cash records together with the records of the depository banks pertaining to the cash accounts of the agency to detect instantly errors either by the bank, Accounting unit or Cash unit; and
make the necessary adjustment for the P 0.01 noted deficiency and other reconciling items and provide a written explanation on funds with negative balances.

We recommended that the LGU’s accounting unit reconcile the discrepancies noted and make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts.
We recommended that the Acting Municipal Mayor create an inventory committee to conduct complete inventory count of all PPE at least once a year in accordance with regulations.

We 	recommended that the Municipal Accountant always prepare financial statements that are accurate and reliable to meet the needs and expectations of its prospective users.  Adjustments for errors and misrepresentations detected during the period are to be effected immediately.

We recommended that management observe proper disclosures in the Notes to FS, particularly on reconciliation of cash and cash equivalents figure per SCF with that in the SFPos, reconciliation of net cash flows from operating activities to surplus (deficit), and reconciliation of budget and actual amounts with that in the SFP.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to disclose the details and amount of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements using the given format above as provided in COA Circular 2012-002. Also, management is advised to exercise utmost diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities concerning the preparation and reporting of financial information that may affect the users in making sound judgments and economic decisions.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the collections regularly and intact to avoid misuse of government funds, pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on the NGAS-LGU. Further, for the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately look into these negative balances and make the necessary adjustments to correct the said deficiency.

We recommended that management, on regular basis:
Provide the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year with a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year;
Inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and
Prepare and maintain a monthly project monitoring report of the PPAs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive closely monitor the utilization of the 20% EDF; ensure that it is utilized to finance the LGU’s priority development projects that is supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program and refrain from allowing charges and/or obligations contrary to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-138 dated December 2.

We recommended that management submit a written explanation and justification on the deficiencies noted above especially on items “c” and “d”. Furthermore, all stages in the procurement process be faithfully adhered to in line with the commitment of the Government to promote good governance and its effort to adhere to the principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency, and economy in procurement.

We recommended that management:
Justify the non-submission of the above cited contracts and its supporting documents within the prescribed period;
Submit the complete set of documents pertaining to the above-cited projects and of all other projects implemented in CY 2019 according to the sequence cited in the schedule above with proper tabbing for easy referencing; and
Strictly observed the submission of required documents within the prescribed reglementary period for all future procurements for timely, systematic, and effective review and evaluation of the project.

We recommended that the officials responsible for the preparation and issuance of POs submit the same within the prescribed period and notify the Auditor within twenty-four (24) hours after deliveries of procured items to enable the Auditor to conduct timely inspections of the same.

We recommended the following to management:
Justify in writing the LGU’s action of allowing Job Order personnel to attend trainings, seminars, meetings, and other similar activities as well as the grant of travel allowances in relation to their travels.
Clearly indicate in the Job Order contract the term or duration of the services to be rendered as well as the allowed benefits or allowances that these personnel can claim; and
Ensure that long term program for LGU personnel at the expense of the government be granted to regular employees and not to personnel whose services are for short duration only.

We recommended that management:
Strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely submission of the GPB and AR to the COA Audit Team;
Require the GAD Focal Point System to: (i) institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; (ii) undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and (iii) revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues; and
Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
We recommended that management observe proper monitoring of taxes withheld and its remittances. Explain in writing the cause of the noted deficiency.
We recommended that management observe proper monitoring of GSIS contribution and its remittances. Explain in writing the cause of the noted deficiency.

We recommend that management submit the said lacking documents in order to determine the propriety and veracity of the transaction. In addition, we further recommend that management observed proper internal control and stop allowing disbursements with lacking supporting documents.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to use the prescribed presentation of discussion of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements as provided in COA Circular 2012-002 and to make necessary corrections and/or adjustments as the case maybe on the mathematical errors observed in the “Balance” column. Lastly, the management is advised to exercise utmost diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities concerning the preparation and reporting of financial information that may affect the users in making sound judgments and economic decisions.

We recommend that Municipal DRRM Officer coordinates with the Municipal Accountant to ensure the timely preparation and submission of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that management charge liquidated damages or rescind the contracts of projects or programs when there is significant delay in the completion or delivery of the said projects or programs. Submit necessary documents to justify the cause of the delays or to support the extension of time for the completion of the project, if any.

We recommend to management to adhere to the guidelines in the implementation of BuB projects especially in the creation of a sub-committee that shall serve as the Project Monitoring Committee to fast track the completion of the remaining on-going BuB projects. In addition, we necessitate the LPRAT to conduct meetings regularly to reassure the strong participation of basic sector organization and other civic society organizations.

We recommend that management, on regular basis:
Provide the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year with a list of all on-going government programs/projects/activities (PPA) and those that are to be implemented during the year;
Inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and
Prepare and maintain a monthly project monitoring report of the PPAs.

We recommend the submission of the list of the newly created Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and a copy of the local government solid waste management plans to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation and verification of the systematic, comprehensive and ecological waste management program of the municipal government.

We recommend Management to:
ensure that purchases of supplies and materials for stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period, shall be recorded as inventory following the perpetual inventory method, and charge the same to expense accounts based on appropriate inventory records or documents, such as the Request and Issue Slip (RIS) and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI);
direct the Accounting Unit to maintain perpetual inventory records, such as the Supplies Ledger Cards for each inventory stock.  The subsidiary ledger cards shall contain the details of the General Ledger accounts; and
for check and balance, require the General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer, as the case maybe, to maintain Stock Cards (SC) for inventories.  The balance in quantity per SC should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the Accounting Unit.

We recommend management to direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify all completed projects to its appropriate asset account and stop the practice of maintaining these projects in the Construction in Progress account for a long period of time. Moreover, direct the accounting department to observe utmost diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities concerning the preparation and reporting of financial information that may affect the users in making sound judgments and economic decisions and see to it that the financial information are reliable and correct in order to enhance the degree of confidence of intended users in the financial statement.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to record the liquidations made in relation to the various funds received from the National Governments Agencies. Deduct from the Due to NGA account the corresponding amounts already liquidated. We recommend further that management stop the practice of maintaining these funds as liabilities for a long period of time and observe utmost diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities concerning the preparation and reporting of financial information. Moreover, direct the accounting department to return to the source agency any unused balance of the funds upon completion as prescribed by the above cited provision.

We recommend that the Disbursing Officer maintains and updates separate Cashbooks - Cash Advances” for each account and for each sources of funds to record the cash advances received and disbursements made out of the cash advances. Ensure that all monies received are correctly recorded and fully accounted for in accordance with laws, rules and regulations. Regular reconciliation of records between the Accounting Section and the Accountable Officer is further recommended at least quarterly in order that any error in recording can immediately be corrected.

We recommend to management that adherence to the policies on the grant of cash advances should be strictly followed and refrain from allowing grant of cash advances to personnel with existing unliquidated cash advances.

We recommend that management:
Instruct the accounting section to adopt the Imprest Fund System in handling and recording the Petty Cash Fund, and
Instruct the Petty Cash Custodian to fully liquidate his/her accountability and return any unused petty cash fund at year-end.

We recommend that management direct all its accountable officers to remit/turn-over their collections intact at the close of each business day to the Liquidating Officer together with their Report of Collections and Deposits/Remittances. Furthermore, instruct the accounting section to strictly review reports pertaining to collections and deposits submitted by the accounting to properly recognize related income and other accounts. Also, instruct the accounting section to check whether adjustments pertaining to the above-mentioned collections were already made, and if not, direct the latter to immediately record the necessary adjusting entry as follow:

Debit: Related Income Account			13,445.00
Credit: Prior Years Adjustment			13,445.00

We recommend management to direct the accountable officer to remit or turn-over daily or at the close of each business day all his collections, or where collections are minimal and daily deposit thereof becomes costly and impractical, the collecting officer shall remit their collections at least once a week, or as soon as the collections reach ₱10,000.00.

We recommend to management the following:
Strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely submission of the said reports to the COA Audit Team.
Require the GAD Focal Point System to: (i) institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; (ii) undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and (iii) revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues; and
Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and record adjustments for valid reconciling items. The management should likewise make representations to the respective banks for the correction of bank errors noted in the BRS. Also, the Municipal Treasurer should oversee the bank accounts maintained by the Municipality to avoid any deficiency in the maintaining balance and/or avoid dormancy of accounts that would result to bank charges.

We recommend proper and faithful adherence to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR for all procurement activities so as not to defeat the purpose of the law to have a judicious use of the government resources and ascertain the legality, validity and propriety of transactions to prevent misuse, loss or wastage of government resources.
